title: penstemon atropurpureus
intro: Penstemon atropurpureus, also known as the purple penstemon, is a perennial flowering plant native to the dry woodlands of eastern North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation and acting as an antiseptic, making it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Penstemon atropurpureus can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to calm the nervous system and promote a restful sleep.
